author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! hena dina hoibe āmāra
du̐hu aṅga paraśiba, du̐hu aṅga nirakhiba,
sevana kariba dohākāra

mālā gā̐thi’ diba nānā phule
kanaka-sampūṭa kari’, karpūra tāmbula pūri’,
jogāiba adharayugale

rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa vṛndāvana, ei mora prāṇa-dhana,
ei mora jīvana⋅upāya
jaya patita-pāvana, deha more ei dhana,
tomā vine anya nāhi bhāya

śrī⋅guru karuṇā⋅sindhu, adhama janāra bandhu,
lokanātha lokera jīvana
hā hā! prabhu kara dayā, deha more padachāyā,
narottama loila śaraṇa
en_translation:
Hari Hari! Such a day will be mine. I will touch the limbs of Both, I will see the limbs of Both, I will serve Both of Them.

Stringing garlands of various flowers, I will give them. Making a golden box, filling it with camphor and betel nuts, I will supply it to Their lips.

Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, Vṛndāvana—this is the treasure of my life, this is the means of my life. Glory to the Savior of the Fallen! Give me this wealth; nothing else shines for me without You.

Śrī Guru, ocean of mercy, friend of the fallen people, Lokanātha—the life of the people. Alas, alas! Master, be merciful, give me the shade of your feet; Narottama has taken shelter.
